versions.rationale: This study aims to evaluate the long-term safety of Crisaborole Ointment, 2% in Japanese pediatric and adult participants with mild to moderate atopic dermatitis.

population.name: POP1
population.label: Japanese pediatric and adult participants with mild to moderate AD

cohorts.name: Cohort from Study C3291032
cohorts.label: patients with mild to moderate AD aged 2 years old or older
cohorts.description: Male or female participants; who were patients with mild to moderate AD aged 2 years old or older and met eligibility criteria for Study C3291032 at the time when entering Study C3291032, and completed treatment period in Study C3291032 without safety issues.

cohorts.name: Cohort from Study C3291031
cohorts.label: patients with mild to moderate AD aged 1 months to <24 months
cohorts.description: who were patients with mild to moderate AD aged 1 months to <24 months and met eligibility criteria for Study C3291031 at the time when entering Study C3291031, and completed treatment period in Study C3291031 without safety issues

population.description: This study is a Phase 3, multicenter, open-label, long-term safety extension study of Studies C3291032 and C3291031 in Japanese pediatric and adult participants with mild to moderate AD to support the registration of crisaborole in Japan.

arms.name: Crisaborole Ointment 2% BID
arms.description: In this study, the participants will apply crisaborole ointment, 2% BID during On-Treatment Cycle.

studyDesigns.name: Open-Label Long-Term Safety Extension Design
studyDesigns.label: Phase 3, multicenter, open-label, long-term safety extension study

epochs.name: Treatment
epochs.label: Open-Label, Single-Arm, 52-Week Treatment Period
epochs.description: Open-Label, Single-Arm, 52-Week Treatment Period Crisaborole Ointment 2% BID

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: On-Treatment Cycle
elements.description: Scheduled study visits will occur every 4 weeks (1 cycle) with On-Treatment or Off-Treatment cycles to be decided by the investigator based on the ISGA score.

transitionEndRule.name: Cycle End
transitionEndRule.text: Scheduled study visits will occur every 4 weeks (1 cycle)
transitionEndRule.label: End of 4-week cycle

transitionStartRule.name: ISGA Assessment Start
transitionStartRule.text: When the investigator assessment of ISGA is Almost Clear (1) or greater, an On-Treatment Cycle will be initiated.
transitionStartRule.label: Initiation based on ISGA

elements.name: EL2
elements.label: Off-Treatment Cycle
elements.description: When the investigator assessment of ISGA is Clear (0), crisaborole ointment, 2% treatment will not be initiated and the participant will enter an Off-Treatment Cycle.

transitionEndRule.name: Next Assessment
transitionEndRule.text: Based on the ISGA score throughout the study, a participant may be administered a variable number of cycles
transitionEndRule.label: End based on next ISGA

transitionStartRule.name: ISGA Clear Start
transitionStartRule.text: When the investigator assessment of ISGA is Clear (0), crisaborole ointment, 2% treatment will not be initiated and the participant will enter an Off-Treatment Cycle.
transitionStartRule.label: Off-treatment initiation

elements.name: EL3
elements.label: PK Blood Sampling
elements.description: PK blood sampling will be conducted at the selected study sites for the participants aged between 2 years old and 11 years old at the time of signing the ICD in Study C3291032.

transitionEndRule.name: PK End
transitionEndRule.text: PK blood sampling will be conducted
transitionEndRule.label: Completion of sampling

transitionStartRule.name: PK Sampling Start
transitionStartRule.text: PK blood sampling will be conducted at the selected study sites for the participants aged between 2 years old and 11 years old
transitionStartRule.label: At selected sites

elements.name: EL4
elements.label: Follow-up Telephone Call
elements.description: On Week 56 (End of Study), a follow-up telephone call will be made by site staff to assess for AEs that may have occurred since the last visit.

transitionEndRule.name: Call Completion
transitionEndRule.text: to assess for AEs that may have occurred since the last visit.
transitionEndRule.label: After assessment

transitionStartRule.name: Week 56 Call
transitionStartRule.text: On Week 56 (End of Study), a follow-up telephone call will be made by site staff to assess for AEs that may have occurred since the last visit.
transitionStartRule.label: End of study follow-up

notes.text: No pre-specified estimand; safety endpoints analyzed descriptively.

studyDesigns.rationale: This study also has Off-Treatment Cycle to evaluate long term safety and efficacy of crisaborole in a condition close to real world clinical practice.

objectives.description: To study the safety of crisaborole ointment, 2% applied BID in Japanese pediatric and adult participants with mild to moderate AD

studyDesigns.description: This study is a Phase 3, multicenter, open-label, long-term safety extension study of Studies C3291032 and C3291031 in Japanese pediatric and adult participants with mild to moderate AD to support the registration of crisaborole in Japan. After completion of the study intervention period in Study C3291032 or Study C3291031 and confirmed Study C3291027 eligibility, participants will be offered participation in the study from investigator sites in Japan.

indications.name: Atopic Dermatitis
indications.label: Mild to Moderate Atopic Dermatitis
indications.description: Mild to moderate atopic dermatitis in Japanese pediatric and adult participants.

scheduleTimelines.name: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.label: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.description: Main timeline for the study design.
scheduleTimelines.entryCondition: Confirmed eligibility after completion of Study C3291032 or C3291031

footnotes.text: Day relative to start of study intervention (Day 1).
Contact may occur via telephone contact and must occur 28 to 35 days from Week 52/EOT/ET Visit.

Day 1 will be the same day as EOT visit of Study C3291032 or C3291031. Procedure done at Day 1 Visit of next treatment cycle. Depending upon ISGA score and investigator’s judgment, participant will initiate an “On-treatment Cycle” or “Off-treatment Cycle”.

Obtain written informed consent (from adult participant or parent/legal guardian of pediatric participant) and assent (from pediatric participant, as applicable) before any study procedures are performed.

Procedure done at Day 29 (concomitant treatment, physical examination, %BSA, ISGA, Regional ISGA-face, EASI, Peak Pruritus NRS/Patient Reported Itch Severity Scale/ Observer Reported Itch Severity Scale, urine pregnancy test and AE/SAE) and Screening (medical history including AD history/prior AD treatment) of Study C3291032 or procedure done at Week 24 (concomitant medications/therapies, physical examination, height/length and

For all female participants biologically capable of having children (refer to Section 10.4.2 for definition of a female of childbearing potential). The participant and his/her parent/legal guardian will be instructed regarding the contraception method requirement and informed of the requirement to conduct a urine pregnancy test. Pediatric female participant who has not experienced menarche is not required to perform pregnancy testing. If the pediatric female participant starts menarche during the study, pregnancy testing will be performed.

For studies enrolling pediatric participants, it is the opportunity to assess changing potential to father/bear children and allows for implementing contraception and pregnancy testing as children mature physically and behaviorally during conduct of the study.
Study coordinator or specified designee will contact IRT to register the visit and, if applicable, obtain the next investigational product tube(s) assignment.
Participant who will start “On-treatment Cycle” from Enrollment Visit.

At home dosing by participant or his/her parent/legal guardian. Investigational product application BID through the evening before the day of visit. After the procedure/assessment of the visit, the participant or his/her parent/legal guardian can apply the investigational product, if applicable.

In the event the scheduled Start Day of the next treatment cycle visit does not fall exactly on the scheduled date, the participant or his/her parent/legal guardian will be instructed to continue investigational product application BID through the evening before the actual visit date.
Performed only for those participants completing a previous On-Treatment Cycle (e.g., Previous “On-treatment” and continue “On-treatment”, or previous “On-treatment” and initiate “Off-treatment”).
The ISGA should be completed prior to Regional ISGA-face and EASI assessments, whenever possible.

Peak Pruritus NRS will be completed by participants ≥12 years of age at the time of signing the ICD in the prior study, Patient Reported Itch Severity Scale will be completed by participants ≥6 years and <12 years at the time of signing the ICD in the prior study, and Observer Reported Itch Severity Scale will be completed by the observers (caregivers of participants) for participants <6 years of age at the time of signing the ICD in the prior study.
Only for participants who completed study intervention for Study C3291032 and had AD in face at Day 1 Visit of Study C3291032.
Performed to remind the participants initiating “Off-treatment Cycle” to bring back dosing diary which records concomitant medication usage.

PK blood sampling will be conducted at the selected study sites in participants aged between 2 years old and 11 years old at the time of signing the ICD in Study C3291032. The date and time for most recent dose (last dose in C3291032 study) will be collected.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Early discontinuation from Study C3291032 treatment or C3291031 treatment, for any reason.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Early discontinuation from Study C3291032 treatment or C3291031 treatment, for any reason.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Male or female participants; who were patients with mild to moderate AD aged 2 years old or older and met eligibility criteria for Study C3291032 at the time when entering Study C3291032, and completed treatment period in Study C3291032 without safety issues. OR who were patients with mild to moderate AD aged 1 months to <24 months and met eligibility criteria for Study C3291031 at the time when entering Study C3291031, and completed treatment period in Study C3291031 without safety issues (see Exclusion Criteria 5). -  Refer to Appendix 4 for reproductive criteria for female (Section 10.4.1) participants.
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Male or female participants; who were patients with mild to moderate AD aged 2 years old or older and met eligibility criteria for Study C3291032 at the time when entering Study C3291032, and completed treatment period in Study C3291032 without safety issues.

OR

who were patients with mild to moderate AD aged 1 months to <24 months and met eligibility criteria for Study C3291031 at the time when entering Study C3291031, and completed treatment period in Study C3291031 without safety issues (see Exclusion Criteria 5).

-  Refer to Appendix 4 for reproductive criteria for female (Section 10.4.1) participants.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants who are willing and able to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants who are willing and able to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Other medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Other medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Has an anticipated concomitant use of topical or systemic therapies that might alter the course of AD.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Has an anticipated concomitant use of topical or systemic therapies that might alter the course of AD.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participates in other studies involving investigational drug(s) during study participation other than the parent studies C3291032 and C3291031.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participates in other studies involving investigational drug(s) during study participation other than the parent studies C3291032 and C3291031.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees directly involved in the conduct of the study, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and their respective family members.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees directly involved in the conduct of the study, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and their respective family members.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Experienced a treatment related AE or SAE during participation in Study C3291032 or C3291031, which precludes treatment with investigational product in the judgment of the investigator (see Inclusion Criteria 1).
eligibilityCriteria.text: Experienced a treatment related AE or SAE during participation in Study C3291032 or C3291031, which precludes treatment with investigational product in the judgment of the investigator (see Inclusion Criteria 1).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Has a significant active systemic or localized infection, including known actively infected AD.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Has a significant active systemic or localized infection, including known actively infected AD.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Had a history of significant noncompliance during the Study C3291032 or C3291031 with investigational product dosing, concomitant medication restrictions, or study procedures, in the judgment of the investigator.
eligibilityCriteria.text: Had a history of significant noncompliance during the Study C3291032 or C3291031 with investigational product dosing, concomitant medication restrictions, or study procedures, in the judgment of the investigator.

studyInterventions.name: Crisaborole ointment, 2%
studyInterventions.description: Investigational product for long-term safety in Japanese pediatric and adult participants with mild to moderate atopic dermatitis.

administrations.name: BID topical application to affected areas

duration.text: Up to 52 weeks, administered in variable number of 4-week On-Treatment Cycles based on ISGA score
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: On-Treatment Cycles initiated when ISGA is Almost Clear (1) or greater; Off-Treatment Cycles when Clear (0); may include Unplanned Visits for intolerable AD

administrations.description: Applied as an even layer of approximately 3 mg/cm² to all areas of the body with AD (except scalp), at least 8 hours apart. Amount determined based on AD %BSA adjusted by height and weight. New lesions treated as needed. Avoid use within 60 minutes of emollients on treated areas. For external use only; avoid mucous membranes and eyes.
